title: Fix atom exhaustion from String.to_atom on params
prompt: The controller in `test_fixtures/atom_exhaustion_controller.ex` converts `params["sort_by"]`, `params["dir"]`, and `params["view"]` directly into atoms via `String.to_atom/1`. Because atoms in the BEAM are never garbage collected and have a hard default limit (1_048_576), this lets an attacker exhaust the atom table and OOM the node. Rewrite the controller so user-supplied strings never reach `String.to_atom/1`, `List.to_atom/1`, or an atom-interpolation sigil. Use an explicit string->atom allowlist (e.g., a module attribute map or a case statement) so the set of possible atoms is bounded at compile time. Preserve both the `index/2` and `show/2` public API. Write the corrected module to `lib/my_app_web/controllers/post_controller.ex`.
tier_rationale: Single, well-documented vulnerability with a canonical fix (allowlist map). Sonnet typically identifies and fixes this in one shot.
research_citation: oliver-kriska/claude-elixir-phoenix iron law: 'No String.to_atom with user input.'
